user.title: Tecom ChallengerPlus
user.description: Configure the Tecom ChallengerPlus integration. The integration always performs one full startup sync after connecting, so inputs, areas, relays, doors and RAS objects populate on reload. The runtime polling options below only control optional ongoing polling after startup.

data.panel_followup_ack_enabled: Enable follow-up ACK for repeated retries

data_description.tcp_role: Use Server only when the panel is configured to connect to Home Assistant.
data_description.poll_interval: Used only for the runtime polling groups you enable below. Startup sync always runs once automatically.
data_description.input_ranges: Optional. Example: 1-16,21-24,49-56. Leave blank to use Input count.
data_description.areas_count: Areas are polled as a contiguous range from 1 to this value.
data_description.door_last_number: Set to 0 to disable doors completely.
data_description.dgp_door_ranges: Optional. Example: 17-20,21-24,33-36. Leave blank to use the first/last door range.
data_description.relay_ranges: Optional. Example: 1-16,21-24,49-56. Leave blank to use Relay count.
data_description.send_acks: Recommended for CTPlus / management software mode.
data_description.send_heartbeats: Recommended for CTPlus / management software mode.
data_description.heartbeat_interval: CTPlus-style event-driven paths generally work best with a slower heartbeat cadence.
data_description.min_send_interval_ms: Higher values reduce traffic bursts but make manual controls feel slower.
data_description.door_status_mode: Only used when runtime door polling is enabled. Startup door polling always runs once.
data_description.door_status_per_cycle: Only used with Round-robin runtime door polling.
data_description.runtime_poll_inputs: Useful for PIRs or other points that do not reliably generate live events.
data_description.runtime_poll_areas: Enable only if you need periodic runtime area status refreshes.
data_description.runtime_poll_relays: Enable only if you need periodic runtime relay status refreshes.
data_description.runtime_poll_doors: Enable only if you want ongoing runtime door polling after startup.
data_description.runtime_poll_ras: Enable only if you need periodic runtime RAS status refreshes.
data_description.panel_ack_delay_ms: CTPlus-style pacing for panel ACKs. Older Challenger panels may behave better with 20-25 ms instead of instant ACKs.
data_description.panel_followup_ack_enabled: Optional extra ACK for repeated identical retries. Leave off unless you are testing whether a second ACK helps clear a stuck queue-head event.
data_description.panel_followup_ack_delay_ms: Only used when follow-up ACK is enabled. Example: 40 ms.
data_description.quiet_mode_enabled: Turn this off if you do not want the integration to suppress recalls during repeated panel retry storms. Disabling it can help troubleshooting, but it also removes the current CTPlus-style backoff behaviour.
data_description.periodic_session_refresh_enabled: When enabled, Home Assistant will periodically send a soft CTPlus re-handshake to help keep long-running sessions healthy.
data_description.periodic_session_refresh_hours: How often to run the automatic CTPlus session refresh. Example: 12 for every 12 hours.

init.title: Tecom ChallengerPlus options
init.description: Update the integration settings. Startup sync always runs once after reconnect. Runtime polling options below are optional and only apply after startup.

data.panel_followup_ack_enabled: Enable follow-up ACK for repeated retries

data_description.tcp_role: Use Server only when the panel is configured to connect to Home Assistant.
data_description.poll_interval: Used only for the runtime polling groups you enable below. Startup sync always runs once automatically.
data_description.input_ranges: Optional. Example: 1-16,21-24,49-56. Leave blank to use Input count.
data_description.areas_count: Areas are polled as a contiguous range from 1 to this value.
data_description.door_last_number: Set to 0 to disable doors completely.
data_description.dgp_door_ranges: Optional. Example: 17-20,21-24,33-36. Leave blank to use the first/last door range.
data_description.relay_ranges: Optional. Example: 1-16,21-24,49-56. Leave blank to use Relay count.
data_description.send_acks: Recommended for CTPlus / management software mode.
data_description.send_heartbeats: Recommended for CTPlus / management software mode.
data_description.heartbeat_interval: CTPlus-style event-driven paths generally work best with a slower heartbeat cadence.
data_description.min_send_interval_ms: Higher values reduce traffic bursts but make manual controls feel slower.

data_description.runtime_poll_doors: Enable only if you want ongoing runtime door polling after startup.

data_description.panel_ack_delay_ms: CTPlus-style pacing for panel ACKs. Older Challenger panels may behave better with 20-25 ms instead of instant ACKs.
data_description.panel_followup_ack_enabled: Optional extra ACK for repeated identical retries. Leave off unless you are testing whether a second ACK helps clear a stuck queue-head event.

data_description.periodic_session_refresh_hours: How often to run the automatic CTPlus session refresh. Example: 12 for every 12 hours.
